mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, labor market slack severely constrains consumer spending growth and signals deep recessionary conditions, causing investors to price in prolonged earnings declines and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence recovery, amplifying aggregate demand growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a negative rank correlation coefficient over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger corporate output, which translates to higher near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while inflation remains elevated, real interest rates increase sharply, compressing equity valuations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as discount rates outpace nominal earnings growth.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in over 60% of cases where FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate profit margins and investor sentiment, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening employment conditions and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the correlation between payroll changes and short-term market direction falls below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising retail sales indicates supply chain bottlenecks and inventory depletion, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with S&P 500 posting positive 60-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
